title: leptospermum polygalifolium var. citriodorum
intro: Leptospermum polygalifolium var. citriodorum, also known as the 'Lemon-scented Tea Tree', is a variant of the Leptospermum polygalifolium species, a flowering plant native to Australia. The plant's leaves and essential oil are used for their medicinal properties, which include antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate cold and flu symptoms. The herbal preparations of Leptospermum polygalifolium var. citriodorum include tea, essential oil, and infusions, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
